description: An error path skips close, so descriptors accumulate until the process hits its limit and new opens fail with EMFILE.
body: Failure mode: a resource is opened but only closed on the success path, so every error or early return leaks a descriptor; under load the count climbs until RLIMIT_NOFILE is reached and open/accept/socket fail with EMFILE, breaking the whole process including unrelated work. Detection signals: 'too many open files' errors; an fd count (lsof, /proc/<pid>/fd) that climbs and never recovers; failures correlated with elevated error rates upstream. Do not raise the fd limit to mask it. Safer fallback: release every resource with deterministic cleanup (defer/finally/RAII) on all paths, set CLOEXEC where appropriate, and verify the fd count stays flat across repeated runs of the path.
